channel.title: Notes from the Practice, Karen Bell PMHNP-BC
channel.description: Occasional writing on culturally responsive psychiatric care, caregiving, and the work of a small group practice in Fort Lauderdale.

item.title: The Plan Belongs to the Patient
item.description: Framing an antidepressant start as a shared experiment with a defined check in has lowered early discontinuation in my practice. Here is how the conversation goes.

item.title: What I Mean by Cultural Humility
item.description: Competence implies a finish line. Humility is closer to the truth. A note for my Nova students before the new course.

item.title: Caregiving While Carrying a Caseload
item.description: On holding professional, cultural, and family weight at the same time, and why I do not pretend any of those layers away to make a day easier.

item.title: Four Minutes That Change a Visit
item.description: The four working questions of a cultural formulation, and how to run them inside the schedule of a real prescribing clinic.
